# preprocessing.curate_data.basic_ts_checks { #spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.curate_data.basic_ts_checks }

```python
preprocessing.curate_data.basic_ts_checks(data, verbose=False)
```

Checks if the time series data has a datetime index and is sorted.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name    | Type                                           | Description                              | Default    |
|---------|------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------|------------|
| data    | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | The main dataset.                        | _required_ |
| verbose | [bool](`bool`)                                 | Whether to print additional information. | `False`    |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> from spotforecast2_safe.data.fetch_data import fetch_data
>>> from spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.curate_data import basic_ts_checks
>>> data = fetch_data()
>>> basic_ts_checks(data)
```

## Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                       | Description                                                               |
|--------|----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [TypeError](`TypeError`)   | If the index is not a datetime index.                                     |
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`) | If the datetime index is not sorted in increasing order or is incomplete. |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type           | Description                                                |
|--------|----------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| bool   | [bool](`bool`) | True if the datetime index is valid, sorted, and complete. |
